page: Amazon Web Services m5zn.12xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8252C processor with an x86_64 architecture, delivering 48 vCPUs across 24 dedicated physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The CPU operates at a high clock speed of 4.5 GHz and includes 24 MB of L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a balanced memory-to-core ratio of 4.0 GB per core. There is no local storage or GPU hardware bundled with this instance.

In benchmark evaluations, the m5zn.12xlarge demonstrates strong multi-core CPU performance, as evidenced by its stress-ng multi-core score and a Passmark CPU score in the top 25%. Memory performance is highly competitive, with the Passmark memory score reaching the top 10% tier and large-block memory read/write bandwidths ranking in the top 25%. Single-threaded gzip compression also registers strong performance. Conversely, single-core CPU benchmarks, static web serving, and database operations fall into the average tier, while LLM text generation speeds represent a performance tradeoff, scoring in the bottom 25%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m5zn.12xlarge offers a high-frequency compute profile and massive 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth without the overhead of local storage or GPU hardware. This makes it a resource-dense option for network-bound and EBS-reliant workloads. It is highly suitable for multi-threaded applications, high-throughput network appliances, compute-intensive general-purpose workloads, and memory-bound processing tasks that do not require local NVMe storage or hardware acceleration.
description: Amazon Web Services m5zn.12xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8252C x86_64 processor with 48 vCPUs, 24 physical cores, and a high clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmarks show strong multi-core CPU and top-tier memory performance, alongside a high baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. However, single-core performance is average, and LLM text generation represents a notable tradeoff, scoring in the bottom 25%. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for high-throughput, network-bound, and multi-threaded workloads that rely heavily on EBS storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5zn.12xlarge is a general-purpose, network-optimized server featuring 48 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR4 RAM, and 100 Gbps bandwidth, delivering strong multi-core and memory performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m5zn.12xlarge is a network-optimized server featuring 48 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and a high-frequency 4.5 GHz Intel Xeon CPU.
tagline: A high-frequency, network-optimized compute platform featuring dedicated cores and high-speed memory bandwidth for demanding multi-threaded workloads.

192.0 GB of DDR4 memory with top-tier bandwidth performance
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth optimized for EBS
Strong multi-core CPU and memory benchmark results
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for network-bound, multi-threaded, and EBS-reliant applications
